project.name: DeepScan
project.description: Training-free framework for visually grounded reasoning in Large Vision-Language Models (CVPR 2026)

nodes.name: Claude Code Settings
nodes.summary: Local Claude Code settings configuration specifying permissions for shell commands, npm/pnpm tools, and Python scripts used in the understand-anything analysis pipeline.

nodes.name: Apache License 2.0
nodes.summary: Apache License 2.0 governing the use, reproduction, and distribution of the DeepScan project.

nodes.name: DeepScan Project Overview
nodes.summary: Main project documentation covering the training-free framework for visually grounded reasoning in Large Vision-Language Models (CVPR 2026), including quick start, setup, component overview, and launch instructions for three specialized services.

nodes.name: BLIP Search Expert Server Launcher
nodes.summary: Bash script that launches the BLIP search expert service for patch-wise Grad-CAM attention map computation, with GPU-aware port allocation (8100 + GPU_ID).

nodes.name: Visual Expert Server Launcher
nodes.summary: Bash script that launches the visual expert service (LangSAM/GroundingDINO) for text-conditioned object detection and segmentation, with GPU-aware port allocation (8000 + GPU_ID).

nodes.name: SAM2 Segmentation Server Launcher
nodes.summary: Bash script that launches the SAM2 point-prompt segmentation service for converting heatmap centroids into binary masks, with GPU-aware port allocation (8200 + GPU_ID).

nodes.name: V* Experiment Batch Runner
nodes.summary: Main entry point shell script for batch evaluation on V* dataset, supporting multi-GPU data parallelism through chunking, merging results, and invoking dataset evaluation.

nodes.name: DeepScan Conda Environment
nodes.summary: Conda environment specification for the main DeepScan runtime (Python 3.10), including Qwen-VL model, vLLM, FastAPI, and comprehensive ML/vision libraries for inference and evaluation.

nodes.name: DeepScan Paper
nodes.summary: The CVPR 2026 conference paper presenting the DeepScan framework (arXiv:2603.03857) describing the training-free approach for visually grounded reasoning.

nodes.name: System Architecture and Method Mapping
nodes.summary: Detailed documentation mapping paper concepts to implementation files, service architecture (BLIP, LangSAM, SAM2), method flow pipeline, and multi-GPU configuration for different setups.

nodes.name: Experiment and Batch Processing Flow
nodes.summary: Documentation explaining the batch experiment runner workflow, dataset sharding, policy dispatch mechanism, async sample processing, and result generation for V* evaluation.

nodes.name: Model Architecture Figure
nodes.summary: Visual diagram of the DeepScan model architecture illustrating the three-stage pipeline.

nodes.name: Sample Evaluation Results Figure
nodes.summary: Visual figure demonstrating sample evaluation results and method performance.

nodes.name: Teaser Figure
nodes.summary: Main teaser figure showing the DeepScan framework overview and key contributions.

nodes.name: Teaser Image
nodes.summary: PNG version of the main teaser figure for the DeepScan framework.

nodes.name: LangSAM Conda Environment
nodes.summary: Conda environment specification for visual expert and SAM2 services (Python 3.11), including LangSAM, SAM2, GroundingDINO, torch, transformers, and detection libraries.

nodes.name: LAVIS Conda Environment
nodes.summary: Conda environment specification for the search expert BLIP service (Python 3.10), including salesforce-lavis, torch, transformers, and image processing libraries.

nodes.name: blip_service.py
nodes.summary: FastAPI service for BLIP image-text matching model that computes attention maps and filters masks based on text-image similarity. Provides endpoints for generating heatmaps and filtering semantically relevant object masks.

nodes.name: AttentionRequest
nodes.summary: Pydantic request model for attention map computation endpoint containing base64 image, question text, and block size.

nodes.name: resize_to_multiple
nodes.summary: Utility function to resize images to multiples of a given block size using BICUBIC interpolation.

nodes.name: genAttnMap
nodes.summary: Generates attention heatmaps using BLIP model's gradCAM, computing cross-modal alignment between image patches and text queries.

nodes.name: compute_attention
nodes.summary: Computes attention map for a single full-resolution image and question query pair.

nodes.name: compute_full_attention
nodes.summary: Computes attention maps by sliding window approach over large images, combining patch-level heatmaps into full-resolution attention map.

nodes.name: filter_masks_api
nodes.summary: Filters object masks by merging semantically similar masks and removing those unrelated to the question using BLIP embeddings.

nodes.name: attention_map
nodes.summary: FastAPI endpoint handler for /attention_map POST requests that returns resized image and heatmap data in base64-encoded format.

nodes.name: model_service.py
nodes.summary: FastAPI service implementing LangSAM model for object detection and segmentation with batch processing, queue management, and error logging.

nodes.name: ImageRequest
nodes.summary: Pydantic request model for /predict endpoint containing base64-encoded image and text prompt.

nodes.name: PredictionResponse
nodes.summary: Pydantic response model containing detected bounding boxes, labels, and segmentation masks.

nodes.name: BatchProcessor
nodes.summary: Manages request queue and batch processing of images through LangSAM model with configurable batch sizes and queue limits.

nodes.name: predict
nodes.summary: FastAPI endpoint handler for /predict POST requests that decodes base64 images and submits them to batch processor.

nodes.name: sam2_service.py
nodes.summary: FastAPI service providing SAM-2 segmentation model with point-based prompting interface using load-once, thread-safe predictor pattern.

nodes.name: PointPromptReq
nodes.summary: Pydantic request model for SAM-2 point prompt predictions with positive/negative points and multimask flag.

nodes.name: PointPromptResp
nodes.summary: Pydantic response model containing binary segmentation mask and IoU scores from SAM-2.

nodes.name: _Sam2Engine
nodes.summary: Load-once, thread-safe SAM-2 model predictor with point coordinate and label handling.

nodes.name: _b64_to_rgb_ndarray
nodes.summary: Utility to decode base64 image strings to RGB numpy arrays using OpenCV.

nodes.name: _ndarray_to_b64_dict
nodes.summary: Utility to encode numpy arrays as base64 with metadata (shape, dtype).

nodes.name: point_predict
nodes.summary: FastAPI endpoint handler for /sam2/point_predict POST requests with executor-based async inference.

nodes.name: __init__.py
nodes.summary: Policy registry that merges policy_map from vstar module and other submodules for dynamic policy loading.

nodes.name: __init__.py
nodes.summary: Dynamic policy loader that auto-discovers and registers all QuestionSample subclasses from current module as policies.

nodes.name: client.py
nodes.summary: HTTP client utility functions to fetch attention heatmaps from BLIP service and segmentation masks from SAM-2 service.

nodes.name: get_heatmap
nodes.summary: Requests and decodes attention heatmap from remote BLIP service endpoint.

nodes.name: get_mask_point
nodes.summary: Requests and decodes segmentation mask from SAM-2 service with morphological dilation post-processing.

nodes.name: control_point_sam.py
nodes.summary: Image processing utilities for extracting control points from heatmaps, including heatmap filtering, centroid detection, polygon sampling, and visualization.

nodes.name: sample_points_from_polygon
nodes.summary: Samples uniformly distributed points along polygon boundary using perimeter-based distance intervals.

nodes.name: filter_heatmap_and_find_centroids
nodes.summary: Converts heatmap to binary mask and extracts connected component centroids above area threshold.

nodes.name: filter_heatmap_and_find_control_points
nodes.summary: Extracts control point groups from heatmap by finding centroids and boundary points via convex hull sampling.

nodes.name: cluster_centroids_for_prompts
nodes.summary: Clusters centroids using DBSCAN and returns grouped prompt points for multi-object segmentation.

nodes.name: get_mask_and_label_from_sam
nodes.summary: Mock SAM function that generates circular mask at given point for testing.

nodes.name: iterative_object_extraction
nodes.summary: Iteratively extracts objects by processing control points and filtering overlapping masks.

nodes.name: visualize_highlighted_regions
nodes.summary: Visualizes heatmap, filtered mask, and control points overlaid on original image using matplotlib.

nodes.name: MCTS.py
nodes.summary: Monte Carlo Tree Search implementation for visual reasoning that explores image regions through repeat_question and zoom_out actions, evaluates answer options using VLM, and employs ensemble voting.

nodes.name: MCTSNode
nodes.summary: MCTS tree node representing search state with image, action history, region coordinates, and expert information.

nodes.name: MCTSQuestionSample
nodes.summary: MCTS-based policy that performs multi-step visual reasoning with expert object detection, image cropping, and ensemble voting.

nodes.name: extract_key_objects
nodes.summary: Extracts key objects from question text using LLM-based parsing and regex filtering.

nodes.name: get_expert_boxes
nodes.summary: Queries expert detection service (LangSAM) for object bounding boxes using async HTTP client.

nodes.name: selection
nodes.summary: MCTS selection phase using UCB score to traverse tree towards most promising child.

nodes.name: execute_repeat_question_action
nodes.summary: MCTS action that zooms to expert-detected region and repeats original question on cropped image.

nodes.name: execute_zoom_out_action
nodes.summary: MCTS action that expands region by 1.5x around current region center and re-queries expert.

nodes.name: expansion
nodes.summary: MCTS expansion phase that executes randomly selected untried action.

nodes.name: simulation
nodes.summary: MCTS simulation phase that checks for presence of key objects using binary yes/no questions.

nodes.name: backpropagation
nodes.summary: MCTS backpropagation phase that updates node visit counts and cumulative values.

nodes.name: single_run
nodes.summary: Executes single MCTS simulation by performing selection, expansion, simulation, and backpropagation.

nodes.name: get_final_answer
nodes.summary: Runs multiple MCTS simulations, collects answers from all tree nodes, performs ensemble voting, and returns final answer.

nodes.name: serialize_tree
nodes.summary: Recursively serializes MCTS tree structure to dictionary for saving to JSONL.

nodes.name: _process
nodes.summary: Main entry point that orchestrates key object extraction and MCTS search for final answer.

nodes.name: ours_mcts.py
nodes.summary: Enhanced MCTS variant (OursMCTS) that uses visual grounding on initial full image to detect and justify candidate regions before MCTS search.

nodes.name: OursMCTSQuestionSample
nodes.summary: MCTS variant with visual grounding preprocessing and object justification.

nodes.name: get_final_answer
nodes.summary: Overrides parent to add visual grounding preprocessing and object justification before MCTS search.

nodes.name: justify
nodes.summary: Validates detected objects by querying VLM and computing union bounding box of confirmed regions.

nodes.name: convert_bbox_to_original_frame
nodes.summary: Converts bounding box coordinates from resized image space back to original image space.

nodes.name: policy.py
nodes.summary: Base abstract policy class for question answering with image processing, OpenAI/Qwen VLM generation, and error handling.

nodes.name: QuestionSample
nodes.summary: Abstract base class for question answering policies with image decoding, VLM client management, and option parsing.

nodes.name: generate
nodes.summary: Generates text from image and prompt using OpenAI-compatible API with load balancing across clients.

nodes.name: generate_local
nodes.summary: Generates text using local Qwen VLM runtime with configurable concurrency control.

nodes.name: process
nodes.summary: Public process entry point with exception handling that delegates to subclass-specific _process method.

nodes.name: visual_grounding.py
nodes.summary: Visual grounding pipeline that extracts candidate objects from heatmap-based region proposals using control points and iterative SAM-2 segmentation.

nodes.name: filter_points_by_mask
nodes.summary: Filters point list by removing points covered by existing mask.

nodes.name: filter_groups_by_mask
nodes.summary: Filters grouped control points by removing groups with centroid covered by existing mask.

nodes.name: calculate_iou
nodes.summary: Computes Intersection over Union between two binary masks.

nodes.name: get_bbox_from_mask_numpy
nodes.summary: Extracts axis-aligned bounding box from binary mask.

nodes.name: merge_overlapping_masks
nodes.summary: Merges masks with IoU above threshold and applies morphological closing.

nodes.name: iterative_segmentation_from_heatmap
nodes.summary: Iteratively extracts and merges object masks by querying SAM-2 for each control point.

nodes.name: grounding
nodes.summary: Main visual grounding pipeline that fetches heatmap, extracts control points, segments objects, and returns cropped proposals.

nodes.name: qwen_runtime.py
nodes.summary: Qwen VLM runtime wrapper providing async image-to-text generation with dtype conversion, processor configuration, and lazy singleton loading.

nodes.name: QwenVLRuntime
nodes.summary: Manages Qwen VLM model and processor with async generation, image preprocessing, and concurrency control.

nodes.name: _to_dtype
nodes.summary: Converts dtype string to torch dtype or auto mode.

nodes.name: _to_pil_image
nodes.summary: Converts string (base64/path/data-url) or PIL image to RGB PIL Image.

nodes.name: _to_pil_images
nodes.summary: Converts single image or list of images to list of PIL Images.

nodes.name: _blocking_generate
nodes.summary: Blocking generation method that runs in executor, processing images and text through Qwen model.

nodes.name: generate
nodes.summary: Async wrapper that runs blocking generation in executor with concurrency control via semaphore.

nodes.name: get_qwen_runtime
nodes.summary: Lazy-loads and returns singleton QwenVLRuntime instance with thread-safe locking.

nodes.name: run_single_sample.py
nodes.summary: CLI script for single-sample inference that loads question data, selects a sample, instantiates policy, and runs question processing.

nodes.name: _select_row
nodes.summary: Selects a single question row from dataframe by row index or index column value.

nodes.name: _ensure_image_base64
nodes.summary: Validates that image column exists and contains non-null base64 data.

nodes.name: run_single_sample
nodes.summary: Main entry point that loads questions, selects sample, instantiates policy, processes across multiple rounds, and saves results.

nodes.name: run.py
nodes.summary: CLI script for batch evaluation that loads question data, parallelizes sample creation and processing, and writes JSONL results.

nodes.name: create_sample
nodes.summary: Creates QuestionSample instance from row data and method configuration.

nodes.name: process_sample
nodes.summary: Processes single sample by calling its async process method.

nodes.name: eval_model
nodes.summary: Main evaluation loop that creates samples, processes in parallel using asyncio, and writes results to JSONL.

nodes.name: utils.py
nodes.summary: Utility module providing OpenAI client configuration, list chunking, null value checking, and image loading helpers.

nodes.name: get_openai_clients_and_models
nodes.summary: Creates OpenAI AsyncOpenAI clients per GPU with local API base URLs for load balancing.

nodes.name: split_list
nodes.summary: Splits list into n roughly equal-sized chunks.

nodes.name: get_chunk
nodes.summary: Returns k-th chunk from n-way split of list.

nodes.name: is_none
nodes.summary: Checks if value is None, NaN float, or string 'nan'/'none'.

nodes.name: get_options
nodes.summary: Extracts non-null option values from row dictionary by letter keys.

nodes.name: load_image_from_base64
nodes.summary: Decodes base64 string to PIL RGB image.

layers.name: Documentation & Configuration
layers.description: Top-level documentation, licensing, and environment configuration files. Define project structure, setup instructions, architectural decisions, and conda environments for different services.

layers.name: Runtime Orchestration
layers.description: Entry points for evaluation and batch processing. Coordinates the full pipeline including sample creation, policy instantiation, parallel processing, and result serialization.

layers.name: Policy & Inference Strategy
layers.description: Core reasoning logic implementing visual grounding and MCTS-based multi-step inference. Includes base policy class, MCTS variants (standard and enhanced with visual grounding), visual grounding pipeline, and control point extraction utilities.

layers.name: Runtime & VLM Interface
layers.description: Wraps the Qwen Vision-Language Model backend with async generation, image preprocessing, and concurrency control. Also provides shared utility functions for OpenAI client management, list chunking, and image loading.

layers.name: Expert Services
layers.description: FastAPI microservices providing specialized vision capabilities. Three independent services: BLIP for attention-based search, LangSAM for detection and segmentation, and SAM2 for point-prompt segmentation. Each includes request/response models, inference logic, and async endpoints.

tour.title: Project Overview and Vision
tour.description: Understand DeepScan's core contribution: a training-free framework for visually grounded reasoning in LVLMs. Learn about the three-stage pipeline (Hierarchical Scanning, Refocusing, Evidence-Enhanced Reasoning) and the CVPR 2026 paper.
tour.languageLesson: Markdown documentation for research project communication and setup instructions

tour.title: System Architecture and Design
tour.description: Explore the overall architecture of DeepScan including the paper-to-code mapping, runtime entry points, service architecture (BLIP, LangSAM, SAM2), and the three-environment design (lavis, langsam, deepscan).
tour.languageLesson: Architectural documentation explaining service-oriented design with HTTP endpoints

tour.title: Environment and Dependency Configuration
tour.description: Learn how DeepScan manages three isolated conda environments with specific dependencies. Each YAML file specifies packages for different expert services and the main LVLM runtime.
tour.languageLesson: YAML configuration for Python environment management with conda

tour.title: Experiment Flow and Batch Evaluation
tour.description: Understand the complete experiment pipeline from shell scripts to batch processing. Learn how run.py handles dataset sharding, async sample creation, policy dispatch, and JSONL output generation.
tour.languageLesson: Detailed documentation of async execution patterns and data parallelism

tour.title: Batch Evaluation Entry Point
tour.description: Explore the main batch runner script that loads question TSVs, instantiates policies dynamically, processes samples asynchronously, and writes predictions. Understand CLI arguments and data sharding logic.
tour.languageLesson: Python async/await patterns, argparse CLI design, and asyncio coroutine orchestration

tour.title: Single-Sample Inference Entry Point
tour.description: Learn the single-sample debugging interface for testing one question at a time. Useful for understanding individual sample flow without batch processing overhead.
tour.languageLesson: Alternative entry point design for testing and debugging

tour.title: Base Policy Abstraction and VLM Interface
tour.description: Understand the QuestionSample base class that defines the policy interface, image preprocessing logic, async LVLM generation, and result formatting. This is the foundation for all reasoning strategies.
tour.languageLesson: Abstract base classes, image processing with PIL, async OpenAI API client usage

tour.title: Core Utilities and Helper Functions
tour.description: Explore shared utilities for dataset loading, data sharding, client management, and common operations used across the pipeline. Understand OpenAI client load balancing across GPUs.
tour.languageLesson: Utility functions, GPU-aware client balancing, data splitting algorithms

tour.title: VLM Runtime Wrapper for Qwen Models
tour.description: Learn how Qwen vision-language models are loaded, configured, and wrapped for inference. Understand GPU device allocation, dtype handling, and multi-image input support.
tour.languageLesson: Transformers library model loading, device management, batch image processing

tour.title: Monte Carlo Tree Search (MCTS) Framework
tour.description: Understand the MCTS algorithm implementation including node definitions, action spaces (repeat_question, zoom_out), reward calculation, and simulation mechanics. This is the core search strategy.
tour.languageLesson: MCTS algorithm, tree node structures, UCB-based action selection with PUCT constant

tour.title: DeepScan-Enhanced MCTS with Grounding
tour.description: Explore the OursMCTSQuestionSample class that implements the complete DeepScan pipeline: hierarchical scanning for cue discovery, evidence justification, refocusing with MCTS, and ensemble evidence-enhanced reasoning.
tour.languageLesson: Policy-specific implementation extending base MCTS with visual grounding

tour.title: Visual Grounding and Heatmap Processing
tour.description: Learn the hierarchical scanning stage: querying BLIP for attention heatmaps, filtering with Otsu thresholding, extracting connected components, and generating candidate evidence regions. Foundation of cue discovery.
tour.languageLesson: Image processing with OpenCV, heatmap filtering, connected component analysis

tour.title: Control Point Extraction and SAM2 Integration
tour.description: Understand how SAM2 point-prompt segmentation is used to convert heatmap centroids into binary masks. Learn polygon point sampling and mask clustering for evidence generation.
tour.languageLesson: Mask processing with NumPy/OpenCV, DBSCAN clustering, binary mask operations

tour.title: HTTP Client for Expert Services
tour.description: Explore how the main runtime communicates with three expert services: BLIP for heatmaps, LangSAM for object detection, and SAM2 for point-prompt segmentation. Understand request/response serialization.
tour.languageLesson: HTTP requests library, base64 image encoding/decoding, numpy array serialization

tour.title: BLIP Search Expert Service
tour.description: Learn the BLIP-ITM based search expert that computes patch-wise Grad-CAM attention maps. This service handles image blocks and returns heatmaps used for hierarchical scanning.
tour.languageLesson: FastAPI service design, LAVIS model integration, Grad-CAM computation

tour.title: LangSAM Visual Expert Service
tour.description: Explore the visual expert service that performs text-conditioned object detection and segmentation using GroundingDINO and LangSAM. Used for localization tasks during MCTS refinement.
tour.languageLesson: Batch processing with async queues, FastAPI request handling, mask serialization

tour.title: SAM2 Point-Prompt Segmentation Service
tour.description: Understand the SAM2 inference service that converts point prompts into binary masks. Handles positive/negative point collections and returns masks used to generate evidence crops.
tour.languageLesson: SAM2 model loading and inference, base64 numpy serialization, point coordinate handling
